title: saturate: A Complete User Guide
text: Annotation: position-linked note attached character range document, distinct coding. Annotations counted frequency analyses. Blind mode: setting Coding tab hides coders’ highlights, allowing independent coding. Case: unit analysis – typically participant, site, organisation. Cases can structured attributes linked documents. Category: lateral grouping codes cuts across code hierarchy. Coder: person applies codes passages. Identified name via profiles. Coding: act attaching code passage text; also refers specific instance attachment. Cohen’s kappa: statistic measuring inter-rater agreement, corrected chance. Values: 0.40 = poor; 0.41-0.60 = moderate; 0.61-0.80 = good; 0.80 = good. Contributor file: copy project created qc_split_project() sent coder independent analysis. Excerpt: labelled passage retained illustrative value, independent code. Krippendorff’s alpha: reliability coefficient handling multiple coders, missing data, different measurement levels. Values 0.80 generally accepted. Master file: central project file managed lead researcher. Member check: Returning summary findings interpretation participant feedback. Merge: Importing new codes, codings, themes, memos contributor file master project. Saturation: point new data stops producing new codes insights. selfirst / selast: 1-based inclusive character positions coding. substr(content, selfirst, selast) extracts coded passage. Split: Creating standalone copy project coder work independently. Theme: higher-level interpretive synthesis multiple codes categories – analytical argument finding.

text: Code document fully moving next. Reading document completely maintains context reduces risk missing important passages. Write journal entry every analytical session. Even two-sentence note felt uncertain decided captures reasoning easy forget. Use provisional codes freely. can always merge, rename, deprecate codes. codebook 60 codes end first-pass coding normal. Validate button identifies unused orphan codes. Run qc_validate_codebook() reliability coding begins. Missing definitions circular hierarchies sources coder confusion – fix first. Take codebook snapshot major milestones. Phase 2, submission, reviewer feedback good moments. Back .satdb file bulk auto-code operation. Auto-code creates many codings ; apply wrong pattern, restoring backup faster removing hundreds codings. Keep attribute names consistent across cases. organisation org treated different variables. using split/merge, keep master file closed contributors coding. requirement , editing codebook master contributor copy can cause name conflicts merge. Lock project finalising dataset. qc_lock_project() prevents accidental writes analysis writing-.

what: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
title: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
text: saturate modern R replacement RQDA package. provides full qualitative data analysis (CAQDAS) workflow backed DuckDB — one portable .satdb/.duckdb file per project — interactive Shiny GUI text coding, codebook management, multi-coder collaboration.

title: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
text: File-based projects — project single .satdb file; server required Rich coding UI — select passages, assign codes, add confidence levels memos Codebook management — hierarchical categories, code relations, weights, full history Multi-coder workflows — split project independent coders, merge results back Audio recording & transcription — record directly browser upload audio, transcribe local whisper, import optional timestamps Saturation analysis — plot theoretical saturation curves triangulation grids Inter-rater reliability — Cohen’s kappa, Krippendorff’s alpha, agreement matrices Member checking — generate track participant feedback interpreted themes Export — DOCX, XLSX, tidy tibbles downstream analysis

title: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
code: library(saturate)  # Create a new project proj <- qc_new("my_study.duckdb", name = "My Study")  # Import documents qc_import_document(proj, "interview_01.txt") qc_import_document(proj, "interview_02.docx")  # Add codes qc_add_code(proj, "empowerment", colour = "#4CAF50") qc_add_code(proj, "barrier",     colour = "#F44336")  # Launch the GUI shiny_saturate(proj)  # When done, close the connection qc_close(proj)

title: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
code: # Researcher splits the master project into a coder copy contrib_path <- "coder_alice.duckdb" qc_split_project(master, contrib_path)  # Alice codes independently, then returns the file. # Researcher merges back: result <- qc_merge_project(master, contrib_path) # list(codes_added = 0, sources_added = 0, codings_added = 47, ...)  # Calculate agreement qc_agreement(master, coders = c("researcher", "alice"))

title: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
code: # Saturation curve qc_plot_saturation(proj)  # Code co-occurrence heatmap qc_plot_cooccurrence(proj)  # Retrieve coded segments as a tidy tibble segments <- qc_get_coded_segments(proj, codes = "empowerment")  # Export full report to DOCX qc_export(proj, "report.docx", format = "docx")

title: saturate — Qualitative Data Analysis for R
text: saturate can transcribe audio recordings directly inside GUI. requires optional whisper av packages: Open Documents tab click Record & transcribe… : Record audio microphone (pause, resume, save raw file) Upload existing audio file (.webm, .ogg, .mp4, .mp3, .wav, .m4a) Transcribe local whisper model (data leaves machine) Include segment timestamps transcript (default) Import edited transcript document, export .txt .docx Whisper models downloaded cached locally. tiny model (~74 MB) works well English; small medium better accented speech languages.

text: Create open project qc_new() / qc_open(). Import documents qc_import_document(). Define codes qc_add_code(). Code passages qc_add_coding() interactively via shiny_saturate(). Retrieve results qc_get_coded_segments(). Close project qc_close().

title: saturate 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. DuckDB-backed project files (.duckdb / .satdb) — one portable file per project. Interactive Shiny GUI text coding, codebook management, multi-coder workflows. Hierarchical codebook categories, code relations, weights, full audit history. Split/merge workflow independent blind coding result reconciliation. Inter-rater reliability: Cohen’s kappa, Krippendorff’s alpha, agreement matrices. Saturation curves, triangulation grids, temporal analysis. Member-checking: generate track participant feedback interpreted themes. Export DOCX, HTML, plain text, XLSX, tidy tibbles.
